caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on liquid yield calculations as part of Tutorial 3. The slide displayed shows a detailed thermodynamic process diagram with various states labeled (1, 2, f) and their corresponding properties such as pressure (p), temperature (T), enthalpy (h), and entropy (s). The liquid yield formula \( y = \frac{(h_1 - h_2)}{(h_1 - h_f)} \) is presented, along with a numerical example where specific values for enthalpy are substituted into the formula to calculate the yield, resulting in \( y = 0.1176 \). The diagram illustrates different paths representing constant temperature (T=const) and constant pressure (p=const) processes, highlighting the transition from state 1 to state 2 and finally to the final state (f). The content is delivered by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, indicating a high level of academic rigor and technical detail.
